humanities_role_explanation: Uses multicultural art collections as evaluation content AND designed specifically for cultural understanding evaluation across 8 artistic traditions including Chinese, Western, Japanese, Korean, Islamic, Indian, and others
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Vision-language evaluation combining artwork images with bilingual (Chinese-English) expert critiques. VLMs generate cultural art critiques that are evaluated against expert-written critiques across 225 culture-specific dimensions
analytical_tasks_explanation: Five-layer framework (L1-L5) progressing from visual perception and technical analysis to cultural symbolism, historical context, and philosophical aesthetics. Tasks require deep cultural knowledge including understanding concepts like Chinese qiyun (spirit resonance), Japanese wabi-sabi, Islamic geometric principles, and Western iconographic traditions

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Comprehensive multicultural art critique benchmark for evaluating VLMs across 8 cultural traditions with 225 expert-defined dimensions

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4o, Claude-Sonnet-4.5, Gemini-2.5-Pro, Qwen3-VL-235B, GLM-4V-Flash evaluated on cultural critique generation

llm_is_primary_system.note: Vision-Language Models are the primary systems being benchmarked for cultural understanding capabilities

arts_humanities_data.note: Visual Arts and Performing Arts: multicultural art collections from major museums including Chinese ink paintings, Western paintings, Japanese ukiyo-e, Islamic miniatures, Indian Mughal art, Korean Joseon works, Buddhist murals, and European court art

quantitative_metrics.note: Dimension Coverage Rate (DCR) measuring engagement with culture-specific concepts, layer-wise performance analysis (L1-L5), inter-annotator agreement (Cohen's κ), and cultural-fact accuracy

english_language.note: Paper in English with bilingual Chinese-English dataset

summary: VULCA-BENCH introduces a comprehensive multicultural art critique benchmark with 7,410 image-critique pairs across 8 cultural traditions, operationalized through a five-layer framework (L1-L5) progressing from visual perception to philosophical aesthetics. The benchmark includes 225 culture-specific dimensions and bilingual expert critiques to evaluate VLMs' hierarchical cultural understanding beyond surface-level visual perception.
key_findings: All evaluated VLMs show a consistent 25-40 percentage-point performance drop from visual perception tasks (L1-L2) to cultural interpretation tasks (L3-L5), with Dimension Coverage Rates ranging from 58.2% to 72.4%. Error analysis reveals systematic failures including surface-level terminology use, historical anachronism, and cultural conflation, demonstrating that cultural understanding represents a qualitatively different capability tier from visual perception.
